text: A coffee cup left on a desk with the morning light passing through it. There's something about that particular combination - the way the ceramic glows when the sun hits it at just the right angle, warming the translucent material so it seems almost alive. If there's still a bit of coffee in it, the brown liquid becomes amber, deeper and richer than it looked when you first poured it. The steam rises in those lazy spirals that disappear almost as soon as you notice them, and if you're paying attention, you might catch how the light refracts through the wisps. It's a small, forgotten thing - you've probably left half a dozen coffee cups in various states around your home right now - but there's something perfect about it. It captures a moment of quiet routine, the brief pause between doing things, and all without any effort on your part. You didn't arrange it for beauty. It just happened, and that's what makes it quietly beautiful.
